title: Cannot get list of tags if tag 'latest' does not exist
body:
I was trying to inspect registry contents with skopeo to find out what tags are available. Before inspecting tags it tries to get manifest for tag 'latest' and bails out if there is none. Therefore it is impossible to get the list of tags unless at least one tag is already known.

Not working:

```
# skopeo --debug inspect docker://quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook
DEBU[0000] reference rewritten from 'quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:latest' to 'quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:latest'
DEBU[0000] Trying to pull "quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:latest"
DEBU[0000] Credentials not found
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:latest
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/quay.io
DEBU[0000] GET https://quay.io/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://quay.io/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0000] GET https://quay.io/v2/auth?scope=repository%3Ajetstack%2Fcert-manager-webhook%3Apull&service=quay.io
DEBU[0001] Increasing token expiration to: 60 seconds
DEBU[0001] GET https://quay.io/v2/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook/manifests/latest
FATA[0001] Error parsing image name "docker://quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook": Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook: manifest unknown: manifest unknown
```

Working:
```
# skopeo --debug inspect docker://quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:v0.10.1
DEBU[0000] reference rewritten from 'quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:v0.10.1' to 'quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:v0.10.1'
DEBU[0000] Trying to pull "quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:v0.10.1"
DEBU[0000] Credentials not found
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:v0.10.1
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/quay.io
DEBU[0000] GET https://quay.io/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://quay.io/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0000] GET https://quay.io/v2/auth?scope=repository%3Ajetstack%2Fcert-manager-webhook%3Apull&service=quay.io
DEBU[0001] Increasing token expiration to: 60 seconds
DEBU[0001] GET https://quay.io/v2/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook/manifests/v0.10.1
DEBU[0001] Downloading /v2/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook/blobs/sha256:bb97c40e90ae5aa9ad7ce306df604a6383709e3b37f0ad721a19031c0097d1ee
DEBU[0001] GET https://quay.io/v2/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook/blobs/sha256:bb97c40e90ae5aa9ad7ce306df604a6383709e3b37f0ad721a19031c0097d1ee
DEBU[0002] Credentials not found
DEBU[0002] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration
DEBU[0002]  Using "default-docker" configuration
DEBU[0002]  No signature storage configuration found for quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook:v0.10.1
DEBU[0002] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/quay.io
DEBU[0002] GET https://quay.io/v2/
DEBU[0002] Ping https://quay.io/v2/ status 401
DEBU[0002] GET https://quay.io/v2/auth?scope=repository%3Ajetstack%2Fcert-manager-webhook%3Apull&service=quay.io
DEBU[0004] Increasing token expiration to: 60 seconds
DEBU[0004] GET https://quay.io/v2/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook/tags/list
DEBU[0004] GET https://quay.io/v2/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook/tags/list?next_page=gAAAAABexpmx3Wx9bakUy3NW3UkgnSSsEQiDmw4pV5mxX2079GDN06MZ-oKejJYaCsBTOmTDCpFTMxgbZLZr4WgM7WWxTHAlI-bcivz0L7x2bmF3rEeJZxw%3D&n=50
...
```

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Yes, `skopeo inspect` primarily inspects a single image; there is nowadays a specialized subcommand for tags.

Does 
```console
$ skopeo list-tags docker://quay.io/jetstack/cert-manager-webhook
```
work for you?
I don't see how inspect would work without latest if the user does not specify a tag.
This is because many of the images in the quay repository still use `scheme1.manifest`
I can’t see how the manifest format matters. Yes, `skopeo inspect` requires the user to submit a known-to-exist image.
My skopeo version is 0.1.40 as provided by alpine. It seems that 'list-tags' option was introduced in a later version.
Could you open up an issue/bugzilla with the alpine distribution.  There is not much we can do here.
